_meta.purpose: Stable MMB codes mapped to detector tags and fix suggestions. Lookup is exact-key first, then category prefix. Dynamic codes like date.setMonth or typed-array.fill resolve via the category prefix entry (date.setter, typed-array).

array.fix: Array prototype mutation. Use the ES2023 non-mutating equivalents (`toSorted`, `toReversed`, `toSpliced`, `with`) or spread into a new array.

collection.fix: Map/Set/WeakMap/WeakSet mutation. Build a new collection with `new Map([...m, [k, v]])` / `new Set([...s, v])` and ES2024 set composition methods.

date.fix: Date mutation. See MMB026 (constructor chain) or MMB027 (setter).

date.setter.fix: Date setters mutate the receiver. Use date-fns immutable helpers (`addMonths`, `subMonths`, `setMonth(d, m)`) or, when the file already uses Temporal, `Temporal.PlainDate.from(d).with({ ... })`.

delete.fix: `delete` operator mutates the receiver. Use destructuring rest.

global.fix: Global state mutation. Pass values explicitly.

object-utility.fix: Object utility mutation (`Object.assign`, `Object.defineProperty`, `Object.setPrototypeOf`). Build a new object with spread.

property.fix: Property assignment mutates the receiver. Use `{ ...obj, key: value }` or `arr.with(i, value)`.

reflect.fix: Reflect mutation. Build a new object with spread or destructuring rest.

typed-array.fix: TypedArray mutation outside a hot-path directory. Applies to Int8Array, Uint8Array, Uint8ClampedArray, Int16Array, Uint16Array, Int32Array, Uint32Array, Float16Array (ES2024), Float32Array, Float64Array, BigInt64Array, and BigUint64Array. Move into crypto/codec/image/audio/parser/wasm/canvas/encoder/decoder/simd/webgl/pixel/hash/cipher, or rebuild the buffer through a fresh constructor (e.g., `new Float16Array(source).set(...)`).

web-api.fix: Web API mutation (URLSearchParams, Headers, FormData). Build a fresh instance from the existing entries via `new URLSearchParams([...params, ...])`, `new Headers([...headers, ...])`, or `new FormData()` populated from filtered entries. FormData mutations may be suppressed when the API consumer requires a stable instance reference.

array.copyWithin.fix: `arr.copyWithin(target, start, end)` mutates in place. Build a new array with `[...arr.slice(0, target), ...arr.slice(start, end), ...arr.slice(target + (end - start))]` or use a helper. Most callers should reach for `Array.prototype.toSpliced` instead.

array.fill.fix: Replace `arr.fill(v)` with `Array.from({ length: arr.length }, () => v)`. For partial fills, prefer `arr.map((x, i) => i >= start && i < end ? v : x)`.

array.pop.fix: Replace `arr.pop()` with `arr.slice(0, -1)`. Use `arr.at(-1)` to read the last value without removing it.

array.push.fix: Replace `arr.push(item)` with `[...arr, item]` or `Array.from({ length: arr.length + 1 }, (_, i) => i < arr.length ? arr[i] : item)`. ES2023 `arr.with(arr.length, item)` is read-only and does not extend; spread is the canonical replacement.

array.shift.fix: Replace `arr.shift()` with `arr.slice(1)`. Use `arr.at(0)` or `arr[0]` to read the first value without removing it.

array.splice.fix: Replace `arr.splice(start, deleteCount, ...items)` with the ES2023 non-mutating `arr.toSpliced(start, deleteCount, ...items)`.

arraybuffer.transfer.fix: `ArrayBuffer.prototype.transfer()` detaches the source: `source.byteLength` becomes 0 and every view raises on access. Allocate a fresh buffer and copy via `new Uint8Array(target).set(new Uint8Array(source))` to keep `source` usable. Suppress with `// @claude-allow-mutation -- ownership handoff` when detachment is the intent (worker postMessage transfer list).

arraybuffer.transfer-to-fixed-length.fix: `ArrayBuffer.prototype.transferToFixedLength()` detaches the source and returns a non-resizable copy. Allocate explicitly when you need to keep the original. Suppress with `// @claude-allow-mutation -- ownership handoff` when detachment is the intent.

async-iterable.for-await-push.fix: Avoid `for await (const x of iter) { result.push(x) }`. Use `await Array.fromAsync(iter)` (ES2024) for a non-mutating materialization. For transformation, `await Array.fromAsync(iter, fn)` accepts a mapper in the same call.

async-iterator.helper-collect.fix: AsyncIterator helpers (Stage 3, TypeScript 5.7+ lib.es2025.iterator.async): `asyncIter.map(fn)`, `.filter(fn)`, `.take(n)`, `.toArray()`. Non-mutating and lazy. Prefer over `for await + push` once the runtime targets ES2025.

collection.map.clear.fix: Replace `map.clear()` with `new Map()`. If you must keep the same reference, the file likely belongs in a state-mgmt scope (Immer, Redux Toolkit, MobX).

collection.map.get-or-insert.fix: Stage 4 `Map.prototype.getOrInsert(key, default)` mutates the receiver when the key is missing. For immutable callers use `map.has(k) ? map.get(k) : default` over `new Map([...map, [k, default]])`. Suppress with `// claude-allow-mutation -- upsert into a local cache` for memoization receivers.

collection.map.get-or-insert-computed.fix: Stage 4 `Map.prototype.getOrInsertComputed(key, factory)` mutates the receiver when the key is missing. For immutable callers use `map.has(k) ? map.get(k) : factory()` over `new Map([...map, [k, factory()]])`. Suppress with `// claude-allow-mutation -- upsert into a local cache` for memoization receivers.

collection.map.set.fix: Replace `map.set(k, v)` with `new Map([...map, [k, v]])`. Use Immer/Mutative if you are inside a state-managed scope (allowlisted by the hook).

collection.set.add.fix: Replace `set.add(v)` with `new Set([...set, v])`. ES2024 `set.union(new Set([v]))` is also non-mutating.

collection.set.delete.fix: Replace `set.delete(v)` with `new Set([...set].filter(x => x !== v))` or `set.difference(new Set([v]))` (ES2024).

collection.weakmap.set.fix: WeakMap entries cannot be enumerated, so producing a new WeakMap requires keeping the source keys somewhere else. Reconsider whether a Map fits your case, or move this code into a state-management scope where mutation is allowed.

collection.weakset.add.fix: WeakSet mutation. Same constraint as WeakMap: entries cannot be enumerated, so a non-mutating clone is impossible. Reconsider whether a Set fits, or move into a state-management scope.

date.constructor-chain.fix: `new Date(other.setX(...))` first mutates `other` and then constructs the new Date with the mutated time. Use `addX(other, n)` / `subX(other, n)` from date-fns, or `Temporal.PlainDate.from(d).with({ ... })` if the file already uses Temporal.

delete-operator.fix: Replace `delete obj.prop` with destructuring rest: `const { prop, ...rest } = obj;` and use `rest`.

finalization-registry.construct.fix: FinalizationRegistry callbacks run at unpredictable GC moments. Keep the callback side-effect-minimal. Never mutate shared application state from the callback; spec note: callbacks may not run at all if the registry is collected. Treat the callback as best-effort cleanup.

global.assignment.fix: Assignment to `globalThis.*`, `window.*`, `self.*`, or `process.env.*` mutates global state. Pass values explicitly through function parameters, module exports, or context objects.

iterator.range-replacement.fix: `Array.from({ length: n }, (_, i) => i)` materializes a full array eagerly. Prefer `Iterator.range(0, n).toArray()` (Stage 2) for lazy iteration; many downstream operations never need the full array.

let.could-be-const.fix: This `let` is never reassigned. Change to `const`. If a conditional initial value is needed, use a ternary or `??` instead of a deferred assignment.

object.assign.fix: `Object.assign(target, source)` mutates `target` unless target is a fresh `{}`. Use `{ ...target, ...source }` or `Object.assign({}, target, source)`.

object.defineProperties.fix: `Object.defineProperties` mutates the target. Spread or `Object.fromEntries` the descriptors into a new object.

object.defineProperty.fix: `Object.defineProperty` mutates the target. Build a new object with the property included: `{ ...target, [key]: value }`. If you need accessor descriptors, use a class or factory.

object.group-by-bucket-push.fix: `Object.groupBy(items, key)` returns a record whose buckets are arrays. Pushing into a bucket mutates that array. Use `Map.groupBy` plus a reducer that produces fresh arrays, or `items.reduce((acc, x) => ({ ...acc, [k(x)]: [...(acc[k(x)] ?? []), x] }), {})`.

object.setPrototypeOf.fix: `Object.setPrototypeOf` mutates the target's prototype chain. Use `Object.create(proto, props)` to construct a new object with the desired prototype.

param.reassign.fix: Reassigning a function parameter shadows the caller's value and breaks referential transparency. Introduce a local `const` with a derived name.

pipeline-operator.fix: The pipeline operator `|>` (Stage 2 hack-style) is a non-mutating composition. The hook recognizes it and does not flag the arguments. Use freely once the runtime supports it.

promise.try-replacement.fix: `Promise.resolve().then(fn)` is the legacy way to wrap a sync-or-async function in a Promise. Prefer `Promise.try(fn)` (ES2024): it wraps both throws and returns into a Promise without mutating intermediate state.

property.assignment.fix: Direct property assignment mutates the receiver. Use `{ ...obj, prop: value }`.

property.compound.fix: Compound assignment (`obj.x += 1`, `obj.x ||= y`) mutates the receiver. Use a new object: `{ ...obj, x: obj.x + 1 }`.

property.compound-index.fix: Compound assignment to an indexed element (`arr[i] += 1`) mutates the array. Use `arr.with(i, arr[i] + 1)` (ES2023).

property.computed.fix: Computed property assignment (`obj[key] = v`) mutates the receiver. Use `{ ...obj, [key]: v }`.

property.increment.fix: Postfix increment (`obj.count++`) mutates the receiver. Use `{ ...obj, count: obj.count + 1 }`.

property.increment-prefix.fix: Prefix increment (`++obj.count`) mutates the receiver. See MMB041.

proxy.trap.defineProperty.fix: Mutating Proxy `defineProperty` trap. Prefer copy-on-write: return a new proxy with the descriptor applied to a fresh target.

proxy.trap.deleteProperty.fix: Mutating Proxy `deleteProperty` trap. Prefer copy-on-write: return a new proxy with the key removed via destructuring rest.

proxy.trap.set.fix: Mutating Proxy `set` trap exposes mutation to consumers. Prefer copy-on-write: the trap returns a new proxy wrapping the modified data. When the Proxy is genuinely a mutable facade, document the contract in JSDoc and suppress.

proxy.trap.setPrototypeOf.fix: Mutating Proxy `setPrototypeOf` trap. Prefer copy-on-write or refactor to a class hierarchy.

reflect.deleteProperty.fix: `Reflect.deleteProperty` mutates the target. Use destructuring rest: `const { [key]: _, ...rest } = target;`.

reflect.setPrototypeOf.fix: `Reflect.setPrototypeOf` mutates the prototype chain. See MMB031.

shared-memory.atomics.add.fix: `Atomics.add` is a lock-free increment on SAB. Audit the modular wraparound at the integer bit width.

shared-memory.atomics.and.fix: `Atomics.and` is a lock-free bitwise AND on SAB.

shared-memory.atomics.compareExchange.fix: `Atomics.compareExchange` is the CAS primitive. Confirm the expected and replacement values; consider Atomics.compareExchange in a retry loop with a maximum iteration bound.

shared-memory.atomics.exchange.fix: `Atomics.exchange` swaps and returns the previous value atomically. Audit ordering against the rest of the protocol.

shared-memory.atomics.or.fix: `Atomics.or` is a lock-free bitwise OR on SAB.

shared-memory.atomics.store.fix: `Atomics.store` writes to a SharedArrayBuffer view with sequential consistency. Confirm the ordering is intentional. Consider Atomics.compareExchange when concurrent writers race; consider a single-writer pattern with message-based publish for one-shot updates. Info-level signal.

shared-memory.atomics.sub.fix: `Atomics.sub` is a lock-free decrement on SAB.

shared-memory.atomics.xor.fix: `Atomics.xor` is a lock-free bitwise XOR on SAB.

typed-array.copyWithin.fix: TypedArray mutation. Same guidance as MMB023.

typed-array.fill.fix: Use a fresh allocation: `new Uint8Array(buffer.length).fill(0)` is allowed inside the constructor expression but not as a separate statement. Move binary buffer code into a hot-path directory if mutation is the domain norm.

typed-array.reverse.fix: TypedArrays do not have `toReversed` yet. Same guidance as MMB023.

typed-array.set.fix: TypedArray mutation outside a hot-path directory (crypto/codec/image/audio/parser/wasm/...). Move into one of those directories or rebuild the buffer: `const next = new Uint8Array(buffer.length); next.set(...)` is also a mutation. Prefer constructing the buffer with the final contents in a single allocation.

typed-array.sort.fix: TypedArrays do not have a non-mutating `toSorted` yet. Either move into a hot-path directory or copy first: `const sorted = new Float32Array(buffer); sorted.sort();` is still a mutation but on a private copy.

typed-array.uint8.set-with-offset.fix: `view.set(source, offset)` is the two-arg form: it copies into the receiver at a given byte offset. Common bugs: byte vs element offset confusion, source length overflow, source-destination aliasing. Prefer `Uint8Array.of(...)` or `new Uint8Array(buffer.slice(...))` and concatenate.

uint8.set-from-base64.fix: Stage 3 `Uint8Array.prototype.setFromBase64` and `setFromHex` mutate the receiver. Prefer the static forms `Uint8Array.fromBase64(str)` and `Uint8Array.fromHex(str)` which return fresh instances.

uint8.set-from-hex.fix: Stage 3 `Uint8Array.prototype.setFromHex` mutates the receiver. Prefer the static form `Uint8Array.fromHex(str)` which returns a fresh instance.

wasm.memory.grow.fix: `WebAssembly.Memory.grow` extends linear memory and invalidates existing typed-array views over `memory.buffer`. After grow, recreate every view from the new buffer. Document pre-grow/post-grow boundaries; prefer coarse-step grows to amortize view recreation.

weakref.deref-mutate.push.fix: Mutating through `weakRef.deref()` is unsafe: the GC can collect the target between deref and the call. Hold a strong reference for the operation, or check `const target = weakRef.deref(); if (target) { ... }` and treat absence as a no-op. Prefer non-mutating ops on the dereferenced value.

web-api.dataview.setBigInt64.fix: `DataView.setBigInt64` mutates the ArrayBuffer. Prefer a fresh buffer outside codec hot paths.

web-api.dataview.setBigUint64.fix: `DataView.setBigUint64` mutates the ArrayBuffer. Prefer a fresh buffer outside codec hot paths.

web-api.dataview.setFloat32.fix: `DataView.setFloat32` mutates the ArrayBuffer. Prefer a fresh buffer outside codec hot paths.

web-api.dataview.setFloat64.fix: `DataView.setFloat64` mutates the ArrayBuffer. Prefer a fresh buffer outside codec hot paths.

web-api.dataview.setInt16.fix: `DataView.setInt16` mutates the ArrayBuffer. Prefer a fresh buffer outside codec hot paths.

web-api.dataview.setInt32.fix: `DataView.setInt32` mutates the ArrayBuffer. Prefer a fresh buffer outside codec hot paths.

web-api.dataview.setInt8.fix: `DataView.setInt8` writes into the backing ArrayBuffer. When the call site is not in a codec hot path, allocate a fresh ArrayBuffer of the required size and DataView, then publish atomically.

web-api.dataview.setUint16.fix: `DataView.setUint16` mutates the ArrayBuffer. Prefer a fresh buffer outside codec hot paths.

web-api.dataview.setUint32.fix: `DataView.setUint32` mutates the ArrayBuffer. Prefer a fresh buffer outside codec hot paths.

web-api.dataview.setUint8.fix: `DataView.setUint8` mutates the ArrayBuffer. Prefer a fresh buffer outside codec hot paths.

web-api.form-data.append.fix: FormData.append mutates the instance. Build a fresh FormData from the existing entries and the new field. When the API consumer requires a stable FormData reference (XHR.send keeps a pointer), suppress with `// @claude-allow-mutation -- <reason>`. Confidence: 3.

web-api.form-data.delete.fix: FormData.delete mutates the instance. Build a fresh FormData from the filtered entries. Suppress when the consumer keeps a pointer to the original. Confidence: 3.

web-api.form-data.set.fix: FormData.set mutates the instance. Build a fresh FormData from the filtered entries plus the new value. Suppress with `// @claude-allow-mutation -- <reason>` when the consumer requires the same reference. Confidence: 3.

web-api.headers.append.fix: Headers.append mutates the instance. Use `new Headers([...headers, [name, value]])`. The Fetch API treats Headers as a mutable map by default; always work with a fresh instance to avoid shared-state bugs.

web-api.headers.delete.fix: Headers.delete mutates the instance. Use `new Headers([...headers].filter(([k]) => k.toLowerCase() !== removed.toLowerCase()))`. Always lowercase both sides; HTTP header names are case-insensitive.

web-api.headers.set.fix: Headers.set mutates the instance. Filter the existing entries and append the new value: `new Headers([...[...headers].filter(([k]) => k.toLowerCase() !== name.toLowerCase()), [name, value]])`. Header names are case-insensitive.

web-api.url-search-params.append.fix: URLSearchParams.append mutates the instance. Use `new URLSearchParams([...params, [k, v]])` to build a fresh query.

web-api.url-search-params.delete.fix: URLSearchParams.delete mutates the instance. Use `new URLSearchParams([...params].filter(([k]) => k !== removed))` for a fresh query string.

web-api.url-search-params.set.fix: URLSearchParams.set mutates the instance. Use `new URLSearchParams([...[...params].filter(([k]) => k !== key), [key, value]])` or build the entries array immutably and construct fresh.

web-api.url-search-params.sort.fix: URLSearchParams.sort mutates the instance. Use `new URLSearchParams([...params].toSorted(([a], [b]) => a.localeCompare(b)))`.

svelte.derived-reassign.fix: `$derived(...)` bindings are read-only. Reassigning them throws `state_unsafe_mutation` at runtime. Move the source of truth into a `$state(...)` binding and let `$derived` recompute, or wrap the computation in a function. See https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/$derived.

effect-ts.ref-value-assign.fix: Effect-TS Ref bindings have no `.value` property. Updates go through `Ref.update`, `Ref.set`, or `Ref.modify` and must be `yield*`-ed inside an `Effect.gen` generator: `yield* Ref.set(ref, next)`. See https://effect.website/docs/state-management/ref.

nanostores.computed-write.fix: Nanostores computed stores are read-only derived values. Only the read accessors and subscription methods are public. Mutating methods such as setKey or the value setter bypass the recompute contract. Move the source-of-truth into an atom or map binding and let computed recompute. See https://github.com/nanostores/nanostores#computed-stores.

tanstack.store-state-write.fix: TanStack `Store.state` is a read-only snapshot. Direct writes (`store.state.x = v`) bypass subscribers and break reactivity. Use `store.setState((prev) => ({ ...prev, x: v }))` so the subscription system observes the change. See https://tanstack.com/store/latest/docs/reference/store#setstate.

xstate.non-assign-context-write.fix: XState v5 enforces `assign(...)` as the only context mutation primitive. Direct writes like `context.x = v` from a non-assign action silently no-op because the callback receives an immutable snapshot. Wrap the update in `assign({ x: ({ context }) => v })` or `assign(({ context }) => ({ x: v }))`. See https://stately.ai/docs/actions#assign-action.

vue.shallow-readonly-nested-write.fix: `shallowReadonly(obj)` only freezes the top level. Nested writes (`x.a.b = v`) silently mutate the underlying reactive object. Use `readonly(obj)` for deep immutability, or rebuild the nested branch with spread (`{ ...x, a: { ...x.a, b: v } }`) and assign the result to a fresh ref. See https://vuejs.org/api/reactivity-advanced.html#shallowreadonly.

mobx.observable-outside-action.fix: MobX strict mode (`enforceActions: 'always'`) requires every observable mutation to flow through an action. Wrap the write in `runInAction(() => { store.count = 1 })` or call an `action`-decorated method. See https://mobx.js.org/configuration.html#enforceactions.

recoil.deprecation-pointer.fix: Recoil is unmaintained as of 2024. Migrate to Jotai (atom-based, closest API), Zustand (store-based with simpler boilerplate), or Nanostores (tiny, framework-agnostic). See https://jotai.org/docs/migrations/from-recoil for a Recoil-to-Jotai mapping.
